code_dossier.name: TraceIdFilter.java
code_dossier.description: A servlet filter that extracts trace ID from HTTP request headers and propagates it through the request lifecycle using SLF4J MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context) for distributed tracing and log correlation.

data.detailed_description:
TraceIdFilter is a Spring Web filter component that implements distributed tracing functionality for the auth-consumer service. It extends Spring's OncePerRequestFilter to guarantee single execution per request, even in complex filter chain scenarios.

The filter performs the following workflow:
1. Intercepts incoming HTTP requests before they reach controllers
2. Extracts the "X-Trace-Id" header from the request, which is typically propagated from upstream services or API gateways
3. Stores the trace ID in SLF4J's MDC (Mapped Diagnostic Context), making it available to all logging statements within the request thread
4. Allows the request to proceed through the filter chain
5. Ensures cleanup of the MDC entry in a finally block to prevent memory leaks and context pollution in thread pools

This filter is crucial for observability in microservice architectures, enabling:
- Log correlation across distributed services
- Request tracing through multiple service hops
- Debugging and troubleshooting of distributed transactions
- Integration with centralized logging systems (ELK, Splunk, etc.)

The implementation follows Spring best practices by using @Component for auto-registration and extending OncePerRequestFilter for reliable single-execution semantics.
responsibilities: Extract X-Trace-Id header from incoming HTTP requests for distributed tracing
Propagate trace ID to SLF4J MDC for log correlation across the request lifecycle
Ensure proper cleanup of MDC context after request completion to prevent thread pollution
Enable observability and debugging capabilities in microservice architecture
Integrate with upstream tracing systems by accepting standardized trace ID headers

interfaces.name: doFilterInternal

parameters.name: request
parameters.description: The incoming HTTP servlet request containing potential trace ID header

parameters.name: filterChain
parameters.description: The filter chain to continue processing the request

interfaces.description: Core filter method that extracts trace ID from request header, stores it in MDC, processes the request through filter chain, and cleans up MDC on completion
